 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// TEST: graph-aware stop evaluation (phase 008 REQ-023)
     2	//
     3	// Validates that the reducer's graph_convergence event ingestion + dashboard
     4	// surfacing work end-to-end. The test should fail if the reducer falls back
     5	// to inline ratio math and ignores graph events.
     6	
     7	import fs from 'node:fs';
     8	import os from 'node:os';
     9	import path from 'node:path';
    10	import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
    11	
    12	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
    13	
    14	const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
    15	
    16	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
    17	const reducer = require('../../../sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs') as {
    18	  reduceResearchState: (specFolder: string, options?: { write?: boolean }) => {
    19	    registry: {
    20	      graphConvergenceScore: number;
    21	      graphDecision: string | null;
    22	      graphBlockers: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    23	      metrics: {
    24	        iterationsCompleted: number;
    25	        convergenceScore: number;
    26	      };
    27	    };
    28	    dashboard: string;
    29	  };
    30	};
    31	
    32	const tempRoots: string[] = [];
    33	
    34	function makeTempSpecFolder(slug: string): string {
    35	  // Realpath resolves macOS /var -> /private/var so reducer path handling
    36	  // stays stable in temporary fixtures.
    37	  const projectRoot = fs.realpathSync(fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), `graph-aware-stop-${slug}-`)));
    38	  tempRoots.push(projectRoot);
    39	  const specFolder = path.join(projectRoot, 'specs', 'phase-008', slug);
    40	  fs.mkdirSync(path.join(specFolder, 'research', 'iterations'), { recursive: true });
    41	  return specFolder;
    42	}
    43	
    44	function writeFile(filePath: string, content: string): void {
    45	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
    46	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
    47	}
    48	
    49	function writeConfig(specFolder: string, sessionId: string): void {
    50	  writeFile(
    51	    path.join(specFolder, 'research', 'deep-research-config.json'),
    52	    `${JSON.stringify({
    53	      topic: 'Graph-aware stop fixture',
    54	      sessionId,
    55	      maxIterations: 5,
    56	      convergenceThreshold: 0.1,
    57	      progressiveSynthesis: true,
    58	      status: 'running',
    59	      createdAt: '2026-04-11T00:00:00Z',
    60	      lineage: {
    61	        sessionId,
    62	        parentSessionId: null,
    63	        lineageMode: 'new',
    64	        generation: 1,
    65	      },
    66	    }, null, 2)}\n`,
    67	  );
    68	}
    69	
    70	function writeStrategy(specFolder: string): void {
    71	  writeFile(
    72	    path.join(specFolder, 'research', 'deep-research-strategy.md'),
    73	    `---
    74	title: Graph Stop Fixture
    75	description: Minimal strategy fixture with all reducer-owned anchors.
    76	---
    77	
    78	# Deep Research Strategy - Session Tracking Template
    79	
    80	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    81	## 1. OVERVIEW
    82	Fixture overview
    83	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    84	<!-- ANCHOR:topic -->
    85	## 2. TOPIC
    86	Graph-aware stop fixture
    87	<!-- /ANCHOR:topic -->
    88	<!-- ANCHOR:key-questions -->
    89	## 3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)
    90	- [ ] Does the graph_convergence event override inline stop heuristics?
    91	<!-- /ANCHOR:key-questions -->
    92	<!-- ANCHOR:non-goals -->
    93	## 4. NON-GOALS
    94	None
    95	<!-- /ANCHOR:non-goals -->
    96	<!-- ANCHOR:stop-conditions -->
    97	## 5. STOP CONDITIONS
    98	Stop once graph convergence handling is proven.
    99	<!-- /ANCHOR:stop-conditions -->
   100	<!-- ANCHOR:answered-questions -->
   101	## 6. ANSWERED QUESTIONS
   102	[None yet]
   103	<!-- /ANCHOR:answered-questions -->
   104	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->
   105	<!-- ANCHOR:what-worked -->
   106	## 7. WHAT WORKED
   107	[None yet]
   108	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-worked -->
   109	<!-- ANCHOR:what-failed -->
   110	## 8. WHAT FAILED
   111	[None yet]
   112	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-failed -->
   113	<!-- ANCHOR:exhausted-approaches -->
   114	## 9. EXHAUSTED APPROACHES (do not retry)
   115	[None yet]
   116	<!-- /ANCHOR:exhausted-approaches -->
   117	<!-- ANCHOR:ruled-out-directions -->
   118	## 10. RULED OUT DIRECTIONS
   119	[None yet]
   120	<!-- /ANCHOR:ruled-out-directions -->
   121	<!-- ANCHOR:next-focus -->
   122	## 11. NEXT FOCUS
   123	[None yet]
   124	<!-- /ANCHOR:next-focus -->
   125	<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->
   126	<!-- ANCHOR:known-context -->
   127	## 12. KNOWN CONTEXT
   128	None
   129	<!-- /ANCHOR:known-context -->
   130	<!-- ANCHOR:research-boundaries -->
   131	## 13. RESEARCH BOUNDARIES
   132	- Max iterations: 5
   133	- Convergence threshold: 0.1
   134	- Per-iteration budget: 4 tool calls, 10 minutes
   135	- Progressive synthesis: true
   136	<!-- /ANCHOR:research-boundaries -->
   137	`,
   138	  );
   139	}
   140	
   141	function writeIteration(specFolder: string, run: number, focus: string, nextFocus: string): void {
   142	  writeFile(
   143	    path.join(specFolder, 'research', 'iterations', `iteration-${String(run).padStart(3, '0')}.md`),
   144	    `# Iteration ${run}: ${focus}
   145	
   146	## Focus
   147	${focus}
   148	
   149	## Findings
   150	- Graph convergence evidence is available for reducer ingestion.
   151	
   152	## Ruled Out
   153	- Inferring graph decisions from inline ratios alone.
   154	
   155	## Dead Ends
   156	- Skipping reducer-owned dashboard checks.
   157	
   158	## Sources Consulted
   159	- research/deep-research-state.jsonl
   160	
   161	## Reflection
   162	- What worked and why: Reducer output makes graph decisions inspectable.
   163	- What did not work and why: Inline ratios alone cannot encode graph blockers.
   164	- What I would do differently: Assert the event rollup directly in tests.
   165	
   166	## Recommended Next Focus
   167	${nextFocus}
   168	`,
   169	  );
   170	}
   171	
   172	function writeStateLog(specFolder: string, lines: string[]): void {
   173	  writeFile(path.join(specFolder, 'research', 'deep-research-state.jsonl'), `${lines.join('\n')}\n`);
   174	}
   175	
   176	function makeFixture(slug: string, stateLines: string[]): string {
   177	  const specFolder = makeTempSpecFolder(slug);
   178	  writeConfig(specFolder, `graph-stop-${slug}`);
   179	  writeStrategy(specFolder);
   180	  writeIteration(specFolder, 1, 'Baseline graph stop probe', 'Inspect the latest graph convergence event.');
   181	  writeStateLog(specFolder, stateLines);
   182	  return specFolder;
   183	}
   184	
   185	afterEach(() => {
   186	  while (tempRoots.length > 0) {
   187	    const root = tempRoots.pop();
   188	    if (root) {
   189	      fs.rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true });
   190	    }
   191	  }
   192	});
   193	
   194	describe('graph-aware stop evaluation', () => {
   195	  it('propagates graph STOP_BLOCKED blockers into the registry and dashboard', () => {
   196	    const specFolder = makeFixture('blocked', [
   197	      '{"type":"config","topic":"Graph-aware stop fixture","sessionId":"graph-stop-blocked","maxIterations":5,"createdAt":"2026-04-11T00:00:00Z"}',
   198	      '{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Baseline graph stop probe","findingsCount":1,"newInfoRatio":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":1000}',
   199	      '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","decision":"STOP_BLOCKED","signals":{"blendedScore":0.41},"blockers":[{"signal":"contradictionDensity","severity":"high"}],"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:06:00Z"}',
   200	    ]);
   201	
   202	    const result = reducer.reduceResearchState(specFolder, { write: false });
   203	
   204	    expect(result.registry.graphDecision).toBe('STOP_BLOCKED');
   205	    expect(result.registry.graphConvergenceScore).toBe(0.41);
   206	    expect(result.registry.graphBlockers).toHaveLength(1);
   207	    expect(result.registry.graphBlockers[0]).toMatchObject({
   208	      signal: 'contradictionDensity',
   209	      severity: 'high',
   210	    });
   211	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphDecision: STOP_BLOCKED');
   212	  });
   213	
   214	  it('uses the most recent graph_convergence event when STOP_ALLOWED supersedes an earlier block', () => {
   215	    const specFolder = makeFixture('allowed', [
   216	      '{"type":"config","topic":"Graph-aware stop fixture","sessionId":"graph-stop-allowed","maxIterations":5,"createdAt":"2026-04-11T00:00:00Z"}',
   217	      '{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Baseline graph stop probe","findingsCount":1,"newInfoRatio":0.01,"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":1000}',
   218	      '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","decision":"STOP_BLOCKED","signals":{"blendedScore":0.33},"blockers":[{"signal":"questionCoverage","severity":"medium"}],"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:06:00Z"}',
   219	      '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","decision":"STOP_ALLOWED","signals":{"blendedScore":0.92},"blockers":[],"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:07:00Z"}',
   220	    ]);
   221	
   222	    const result = reducer.reduceResearchState(specFolder, { write: false });
   223	
   224	    expect(result.registry.graphDecision).toBe('STOP_ALLOWED');
   225	    expect(result.registry.graphConvergenceScore).toBe(0.92);
   226	    expect(result.registry.graphBlockers).toEqual([]);
   227	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphDecision: STOP_ALLOWED');
   228	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphConvergenceScore: 0.92');
   229	  });
   230	
   231	  it('defaults graph fields when no graph_convergence events are present', () => {
   232	    const specFolder = makeFixture('default', [
   233	      '{"type":"config","topic":"Graph-aware stop fixture","sessionId":"graph-stop-default","maxIterations":5,"createdAt":"2026-04-11T00:00:00Z"}',
   234	      '{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Baseline graph stop probe","findingsCount":1,"newInfoRatio":0.88,"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":1000}',
   235	    ]);
   236	
   237	    const result = reducer.reduceResearchState(specFolder, { write: false });
   238	
   239	    expect(result.registry.graphConvergenceScore).toBe(0);
   240	    expect(result.registry.graphDecision).toBeNull();
   241	    expect(result.registry.graphBlockers).toEqual([]);
   242	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphDecision: [Not recorded]');
   243	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphBlockers: none recorded');
   244	  });
   245	
   246	  it('consumes the canonical MCP handler output shape (phase 008 P1-01 closure)', () => {
   247	    // Phase 008 P1-01 closure: the MCP handler emits a canonical `score`
   248	    // field at the top level of the graph_convergence event (and mirrored in
   249	    // signals.score). The reducer MUST consume this shape directly without
   250	    // relying on synthetic `blendedScore` aliases. This test seeds the exact
   251	    // shape the handler produces and asserts the reducer reads it verbatim.
   252	    const specFolder = makeFixture('handler-shape', [
   253	      '{"type":"config","topic":"Handler-shape fixture","sessionId":"graph-stop-handler","maxIterations":5,"createdAt":"2026-04-11T00:00:00Z"}',
   254	      '{"type":"iteration","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"Handler-shape probe","findingsCount":2,"newInfoRatio":0.1,"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":1000}',
   255	      '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","decision":"STOP_ALLOWED","score":0.74,"signals":{"questionCoverage":0.8,"claimVerificationRate":0.7,"contradictionDensity":0.05,"sourceDiversity":2.1,"evidenceDepth":3.2,"score":0.74},"blockers":[],"trace":[],"timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:06:00Z","sessionId":"graph-stop-handler","generation":1}',
   256	    ]);
   257	
   258	    const result = reducer.reduceResearchState(specFolder, { write: false });
   259	
   260	    expect(result.registry.graphDecision).toBe('STOP_ALLOWED');
   261	    // The reducer reads signals.score ?? signals.blendedScore ?? 0, so it
   262	    // picks up the canonical 0.74 directly from the handler-shaped payload.
   263	    expect(result.registry.graphConvergenceScore).toBe(0.74);
   264	    expect(result.registry.graphBlockers).toEqual([]);
   265	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphDecision: STOP_ALLOWED');
   266	    expect(result.dashboard).toContain('graphConvergenceScore: 0.74');
   267	  });
   268	});

    69

   100	    // REQ-030 retraction (042 closing audit, F010/F011/F012): the runtime
   101	    // only persists lineage events for `resume` and `restart`. `fork` and
   102	    // `completed-continue` are deferred with an explicit note. The parity
   103	    // test mirrors the shipped contract by asserting the live branches exist
   104	    // and the retraction note is present, rather than pinning the legacy

   137	    expect(autoMatch![1]).toBe(confirmMatch![1]);
   138	  });

   158	});

    15	describe('deep-review reducer and schema contract', () => {
    16	  it('keeps reducer, severity, and release-readiness schemas stable in the canonical assets', () => {
    17	    const contract = yaml.load(

    27	    expect(contract.contract.reducer.enabled).toBe(true);

    29	      'latestJSONLDelta',
    30	      'newIterationFile',
    31	      'priorReducedState',
    32	    ]);
    33	    expect(contract.contract.reducer.outputs).toEqual([
    34	      'findingsRegistry',
    35	      'dashboardMetrics',
    36	      'strategyUpdates',
    37	    ]);
    38	    expect(contract.contract.reducer.idempotent).toBe(true);
    39	
    40	    expect(contract.contract.releaseReadinessStates.map((item: { id: string }) => item.id)).toEqual([
    41	      'in-progress',
    42	      'converged',
    43	      'release-blocking',
    44	    ]);
    45	    expect(contract.contract.outputs.config.pathPattern).toContain('deep-review-config.json');

    47	    expect(contract.contract.outputs.pauseSentinel.pathPattern).toContain('.deep-review-pause');
    48	    expect(contract.contract.outputs.jsonl.pathPattern).toContain('deep-review-state.jsonl');
    49	
    50	    expect(config.releaseReadinessState).toBe('in-progress');
    51	    expect(config.reducer.inputs).toEqual(['latestJSONLDelta', 'newIterationFile', 'priorReducedState']);
    52	    expect(config.reducer.outputs).toEqual(['findingsRegistry', 'dashboardMetrics', 'strategyUpdates']);
    53	    expect(config.reducer.metrics).toEqual([
    54	      'dimensionsCovered',

    58	      'convergenceScore',

    60	    expect(config.fileProtection['deep-review-findings-registry.json']).toBe('auto-generated');

    63	
    64	  it('wires reducer refresh and machine-owned report guidance into both review workflows', () => {
    65	    const autoYaml = readWorkspaceFile('.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml');
    66	    const confirmYaml = readWorkspaceFile('.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml');

     3	// Covers:
     4	//  - Test 1: malformed JSONL triggers corruptionWarnings + non-zero exit unless --lenient
     5	//  - Test 2: missing machine-owned anchor throws a descriptive error
     6	//  - Test 3: --lenient escape hatch preserves fail-open legacy behavior for corrupted JSONL
     7	//
     8	// Fixtures are written to a macOS-safe temp root (realpathSync wraps mkdtempSync
     9	// so /var → /private/var symlinks don't poison path.relative in the reducer).
    10	
    11	import fs from 'node:fs';
    12	import os from 'node:os';
    13	import path from 'node:path';
    14	
    15	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
    16	
    17	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
    18	const reducer = require('../../../sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs') as {
    19	  reduceReviewState: (specFolder: string, options?: {
    20	    write?: boolean;
    21	    lenient?: boolean;
    22	    createMissingAnchors?: boolean;
    23	  }) => {
    24	    corruptionWarnings: Array<{ line: number; error: string; raw: string }>;
    25	    hasCorruption: boolean;
    26	    registry: Record<string, unknown>;
    27	    strategy: string;
    28	    dashboard: string;
    29	  };
    30	  parseJsonlDetailed: (content: string) => {
    31	    records: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    32	    corruptionWarnings: Array<{ line: number; error: string; raw: string }>;
    33	  };
    34	  replaceAnchorSection: (
    35	    content: string,
    36	    anchorId: string,
    37	    heading: string,
    38	    body: string,
    39	    options?: { createMissing?: boolean },
    40	  ) => string;
    41	};
    42	
    43	const tempRoots: string[] = [];
    44	
    45	function makeTempSpecFolder(slug: string): string {
    46	  // Realpath resolves macOS /var → /private/var so internal path.relative stays
    47	  // consistent with validateFilePath's symlink resolution.
    48	  const projectRoot = fs.realpathSync(fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), `review-fail-closed-${slug}-`)));
    49	  tempRoots.push(projectRoot);
    50	  const specFolder = path.join(projectRoot, 'specs', 'phase-008', 'fail-closed');
    51	  fs.mkdirSync(path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'iterations'), { recursive: true });
    52	  return specFolder;
    53	}
    54	
    55	function writeConfig(specFolder: string): void {
    56	  const config = {
    57	    topic: 'fail-closed test',
    58	    mode: 'review',
    59	    reviewTarget: 'fixture',
    60	    reviewTargetType: 'spec-folder',
    61	    reviewDimensions: ['correctness', 'security', 'traceability', 'maintainability'],
    62	    sessionId: 'rvw-fail-closed',
    63	    generation: 1,
    64	    lineageMode: 'new',
    65	    maxIterations: 7,
    66	    convergenceThreshold: 0.1,
    67	    status: 'running',
    68	    createdAt: '2026-04-11T00:00:00Z',
    69	  };
    70	  fs.writeFileSync(
    71	    path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'deep-review-config.json'),
    72	    `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`,
    73	    'utf8',
    74	  );
    75	}
    76	
    77	function writeIterationStub(specFolder: string): void {
    78	  fs.writeFileSync(
    79	    path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'iterations', 'iteration-001.md'),
    80	    [
    81	      '# Iteration 1: test',
    82	      '',
    83	      '## Focus',
    84	      'fail-closed',
    85	      '',
    86	      '## Findings',
    87	      '',
    88	      '## Ruled Out',
    89	      '',
    90	      '## Dead Ends',
    91	      '',
    92	      '## Recommended Next Focus',
    93	      'next',
    94	      '',
    95	    ].join('\n'),
    96	    'utf8',
    97	  );
    98	}
    99	
   100	afterEach(() => {
   101	  while (tempRoots.length > 0) {
   102	    const root = tempRoots.pop();
   103	    if (root) {
   104	      fs.rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true });
   105	    }
   106	  }
   107	});
   108	
   109	describe('sk-deep-review reducer — fail-closed behavior', () => {
   110	  it('reports corruptionWarnings and flags hasCorruption when JSONL is malformed', () => {
   111	    const specFolder = makeTempSpecFolder('corruption');
   112	    writeConfig(specFolder);
   113	    writeIterationStub(specFolder);
   114	    fs.writeFileSync(
   115	      path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'deep-review-state.jsonl'),
   116	      [
   117	        '{"type":"config","mode":"review","sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed"}',
   118	        'this line is definitely not valid json',
   119	        '{"type":"iteration","mode":"review","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"test","dimensions":["correctness"],"findingsCount":0,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"newFindingsRatio":0.0,"sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":10000}',
   120	        '',
   121	      ].join('\n'),
   122	      'utf8',
   123	    );
   124	
   125	    const result = reducer.reduceReviewState(specFolder, { write: true });
   126	
   127	    expect(result.hasCorruption).toBe(true);
   128	    expect(result.corruptionWarnings).toHaveLength(1);
   129	    expect(result.corruptionWarnings[0].line).toBe(2);
   130	    expect(result.corruptionWarnings[0].error).toMatch(/not valid JSON|JSON/);
   131	    expect(result.registry.corruptionWarnings).toEqual(result.corruptionWarnings);
   132	  });
   133	
   134	  it('throws a descriptive error when a machine-owned strategy anchor is missing', () => {
   135	    const specFolder = makeTempSpecFolder('missing-anchor');
   136	    writeConfig(specFolder);
   137	    writeIterationStub(specFolder);
   138	    fs.writeFileSync(
   139	      path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'deep-review-state.jsonl'),
   140	      [
   141	        '{"type":"config","mode":"review","sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed"}',
   142	        '{"type":"iteration","mode":"review","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"test","dimensions":["correctness"],"findingsCount":0,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"newFindingsRatio":0.0,"sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":10000}',
   143	        '',
   144	      ].join('\n'),
   145	      'utf8',
   146	    );
   147	    // Strategy file exists but omits the required next-focus anchor.
   148	    fs.writeFileSync(
   149	      path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'deep-review-strategy.md'),
   150	      [
   151	        '---',
   152	        'title: partial strategy',
   153	        '---',
   154	        '',
   155	        '<!-- ANCHOR:review-dimensions -->',
   156	        '## 3. REVIEW DIMENSIONS (remaining)',
   157	        '- [ ] correctness',
   158	        '- [ ] security',
   159	        '- [ ] traceability',
   160	        '- [ ] maintainability',
   161	        '<!-- /ANCHOR:review-dimensions -->',
   162	        '',
   163	        // No completed-dimensions / running-findings / exhausted-approaches / next-focus anchors
   164	      ].join('\n'),
   165	      'utf8',
   166	    );
   167	
   168	    expect(() => reducer.reduceReviewState(specFolder, { write: false }))
   169	      .toThrow(/Missing machine-owned anchor .*completed-dimensions|running-findings|exhausted-approaches|next-focus/);
   170	  });
   171	
   172	  it('with --lenient / createMissingAnchors the reducer still records corruption but does not block', () => {
   173	    const specFolder = makeTempSpecFolder('lenient');
   174	    writeConfig(specFolder);
   175	    writeIterationStub(specFolder);
   176	    fs.writeFileSync(
   177	      path.join(specFolder, 'review', 'deep-review-state.jsonl'),
   178	      [
   179	        '{"type":"config","mode":"review","sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed"}',
   180	        'garbage line A',
   181	        'garbage line B',
   182	        '{"type":"iteration","mode":"review","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"test","dimensions":["correctness"],"findingsCount":0,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"newFindingsRatio":0.0,"sessionId":"rvw-fail-closed","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-11T00:05:00Z","durationMs":10000}',
   183	        '',
   184	      ].join('\n'),
   185	      'utf8',
   186	    );
   187	
   188	    const result = reducer.reduceReviewState(specFolder, { write: true, lenient: true });
   189	
   190	    // corruption is still reported...
   191	    expect(result.corruptionWarnings).toHaveLength(2);
   192	    expect(result.hasCorruption).toBe(true);
   193	    // ...but the reducer still produced registry output successfully
   194	    expect(result.registry.corruptionWarnings).toEqual(result.corruptionWarnings);
   195	    expect(result.registry.sessionId).toBe('rvw-fail-closed');
   196	  });
   197	});

codex
I’ve isolated a second likely drift point: the review workflows normalize `review_dimensions_json` for the JSONL config row, but the canonical `deep-review-config.json` still appears to keep the raw `{review_dimensions}` template string. I’m checking whether any reducer/reference surface actually consumes `config.reviewDimensions` as a `string[]`, because if so this is more than a doc nit and turns into a real maintenance hazard.

   103	
   104	### Field Reference
   105	
   106	| Field | Type | Default | Description |
   107	|-------|------|---------|-------------|
   108	| mode | `"review"` | -- | Session mode discriminator (required) |
   109	| reviewTarget | string | -- | Path or identifier of the review target |
   110	| reviewTargetType | string | `"spec-folder"` | `spec-folder`, `skill`, `agent`, `track`, `files` |
   111	| reviewDimensions | string[] | all 4 | Dimensions to evaluate |
   112	| sessionId | string | -- | Stable identifier for the current review lineage |
   113	| parentSessionId | string \| null | `null` | Parent lineage reference for restart flows |
   114	| lineageMode | string | `"new"` | `new`, `resume`, `restart`. `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and not emitted by the current runtime |
   115	| generation | number | 1 | Lineage generation number — incremented on `restart`, unchanged on `resume` |
   116	| continuedFromRun | number \| null | `null` | Count of completed iteration records at the lifecycle boundary (set on `resume` and `restart`) |
   117	| maxIterations | number | 7 | Hard cap on loop iterations |
   118	| convergenceThreshold | number | 0.10 | Stop when severity-weighted new findings ratio below this |
   119	| stuckThreshold | number | 2 | Consecutive no-progress iterations before recovery |
   120	| severityThreshold | string | `"P2"` | Minimum severity to report: `P0`, `P1`, `P2` |
   121	| crossReference | object | -- | Core (hard-gated) and overlay (advisory) protocol sets |
   122	| qualityGateThreshold | boolean | true | Whether binary quality gates are enforced |
   123	| specFolder | string | -- | Spec folder path (relative to specs/) |
   124	| status | string | `"initialized"` | `initialized`, `running`, `converged`, `stuck`, `complete`, `error` |
   125	| releaseReadinessState | string | `"in-progress"` | `in-progress`, `converged`, `release-blocking` |
   126	| fileProtection | object | -- | Mutability declarations (see protection levels below) |
   127	| reducer | object | -- | Canonical reducer inputs, outputs, and metrics names |
   128	
   129	### File Protection Levels
   130

 succeeded in 0ms:
   360	
   361	if total_findings == 0:
   362	  newFindingsRatio = 0.0
   363	```
   364	
   365	A new critical finding always signals significant remaining work. The 0.50 floor prevents premature convergence when critical issues are still being discovered.
   366	
   367	---
   368	
   369	<!-- /ANCHOR:severity-weighted-ratio -->
   370	<!-- ANCHOR:quality-guards -->
   371	## 6. LEGAL-STOP GATE BUNDLE
   372	
   373	Deep review treats STOP as legal only when the full review-specific gate bundle passes together. Convergence math may request STOP, but the workflow must still evaluate these 5 gates and persist a blocked-stop event when any gate fails.
   374	
   375	| Gate | Rule | Fail Action |
   376	|------|------|-------------|
   377	| **findingStability** | Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio must all indicate low-yield review churn | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   378	| **dimensionCoverage** | Every configured review dimension must have been examined at least once, with required traceability coverage stabilized | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   379	| **p0Resolution** | No unresolved P0 findings may remain active at stop time | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   380	| **evidenceDensity** | Evidence density across active findings must meet the configured threshold | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   381	| **hotspotSaturation** | Review hotspots must be revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   382	
   383	### Gate Evaluation
   384	
   385	```
   386	function buildReviewLegalStop(state, config, coverage):
   387	  gateResults = {
   388	    findingStability: {
   389	      pass: rollingStop and madStop and state.latestNoveltyRatio <= config.convergenceThreshold,
   390	      detail: "Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio are all below stop thresholds."
   391	    },
   392	    dimensionCoverage: {
   393	      pass: everyConfiguredDimensionExaminedAtLeastOnce(coverage, config.reviewDimensions) and
   394	            coverage.requiredProtocolsCovered and
   395	            coverage.stabilizationPasses >= 1,
   396	      detail: "All configured review dimensions have been examined, required traceability protocols are covered, and stabilization has aged enough to stop."
   397	    },
   398	    p0Resolution: {
   399	      pass: countActiveFindings(state, ["P0"]) == 0 and state.claimAdjudicationPassed != false,
   400	      detail: "No unresolved P0 findings remain and blocker adjudication is complete."
   401	    },
   402	    evidenceDensity: {
   403	      pass: computeEvidenceDensity(state.activeFindings) >= config.evidenceDensityThreshold,
   404	      detail: "Evidence density meets the configured threshold for active findings."
   405	    },
   406	    hotspotSaturation: {
   407	      pass: computeHotspotSaturation(state.hotspots) >= config.hotspotSaturationThreshold,
   408	      detail: "Priority hotspots received enough revisits to satisfy saturation."
   409	    }
   410	  }
   411	
   412	  blockedBy = [name for name, result in gateResults.items() if not result.pass]
   413	  return {
   414	    pass: len(blockedBy) == 0,
   415	    blockedBy,
   416	    gateResults
   417	  }
   418	```
   419	
   420	When convergence math returns STOP, invoke `buildReviewLegalStop()`. If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class blocked-stop decision with `stopReason=blockedStop`, populate `legalStop.blockedBy` from the failing gates, copy the full `gateResults`, snapshot the replay inputs, and attach a `recoveryStrategy` describing the next review action before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
   421	
   422	### Blocked-Stop Recovery Strategy
   423	
   424	| Failed Gate | Recovery Strategy |
   425	|-------------|-------------------|
   426	| `findingStability` | Revisit the noisiest recent dimension and reduce novelty by closing obvious follow-up loops before re-checking STOP. |
   427	| `dimensionCoverage` | Schedule the next uncovered review dimension immediately. |
   428	| `p0Resolution` | Re-open the active blocker path and verify whether the P0 is real, downgraded, or still unresolved. |
   429	| `evidenceDensity` | Re-read weakly supported findings and add concrete `file:line` citations before they count toward a stop decision. |
   430	| `hotspotSaturation` | Revisit undersampled hotspots or adjacent call sites until the saturation heuristic passes. |

name='memory_surrogates'`,\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:4:trigger_phrases:\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:49:- **Schema Management**: sqlite-vec schema v15 (current) with document-type fields, event-based decay and phase-aware columns\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:58:|---> Vector (sqlite-vec)       -> Semantic matches\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:93:| Vector | `vector-index.ts` | Semantic similarity via sqlite-vec through the split vector-index modules |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:95:| FTS5 | `sqlite-fts.ts` | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:107:4. Graph signals (N2a + N2b) — momentum + causal depth\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:111:8. Feedback signals — learned trigger boosts (0.7x weight) + negative demotions\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:143:- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:177:- `lexicalPath`: the lane that actually ran for lexical retrieval. Current packet-owned values are `fts5` and `unavailable`. The wider response schema also allows `like`, but packet `010` does not claim that lane for this runtime seam.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:184:| `ok` | FTS5 compile probe passed, `memory_fts` exists, and BM25 ranking executed normally | `fts5` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:187:| `no_such_module_fts5` | The SQLite engine rejects FTS5 usage with `no such module: fts5` | `unavailable` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:297:| `causal-boost.ts`          | -      | TypeScript | Causal-neighbor score boosting for graph traversal  |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:298:| `session-boost.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Session-attention score boosting                    |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:311:| `sqlite-fts.ts`            | -      | TypeScript | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring, extracted from hybrid-search for independent use |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:319:| `learned-feedback.ts`      | -      | TypeScript | Selection-based relevance feedback with learned triggers (R11) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:347:| `session-state.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Tracks per-session search state and retrieval context |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:348:| `session-transition.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Handles session-state transition rules during retrieval flows |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:383:   MMR diversity reranking -> Reduce near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:427:| `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS`   | `true`  | Enable N2a momentum + N2b causal depth scoring |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:451:| v3      | Add `related_memories` column to `memory_index`                              |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:456:| v8      | `causal_edges` table with 6 relationship types (REQ-012, T043-T047)          |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:475:// Float32Array -> Buffer conversion for sqlite-vec\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:612:**Purpose**: Create causal edges between memories that share extracted entities across spec folders. Gated via `SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING`. Requires R10 (`SPECKIT_AUTO_ENTITIES`) to also be enabled.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:620:| **Edge Creation** | Creates `supports` causal edges between memories sharing entities |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:631:| `createEntityLinks` | `(db, matches) => EntityLinkResult` | Create causal edges for cross-doc matches |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:646:| `caused` | 1.0 | Strongest causal signal |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:650:| `supersedes` | 0.6 | Version supersession |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:656:Top-ranked results seed a 2-hop BFS traversal over causal edges. Discovered neighbors receive a score boost with `SEED_FRACTION=0.25` strength. A `1/sqrt(neighbor_count)` fan-effect divisor (R17) prevents hub nodes from appearing in every co-activation result.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:662:The causal graph is condensed into strongly connected components, then longest-path depth is computed across the resulting DAG. Shortcut edges do not collapse deeper chains; cycle members share one bounded depth layer, and the final depth is normalized by the deepest reachable component chain (`maxDepth`). Score bonus: `normalizedDepth * 0.05` — rewards structurally deep nodes in the causal chain.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:673:Processing steps applied during `memory_save` before a memory is persisted.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:687:| 2. Content Quality | 5-dimension weighted signal density | Dimensions: title (0.25), triggers (0.20), length (0.20), anchors (0.15), metadata (0.20) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:688:| 3. Semantic Dedup | Cosine similarity against existing memories | Threshold: 0.92 rejects near-duplicates |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:698:| 0.75 - 0.88 | **Conflict** | Supersede: deprecate existing, create `supersedes` causal edge |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:719:`buildBm25DocumentText()` builds the canonical lexical document from title, content, trigger phrases, and folder metadata. `normalizeLexicalQueryTokens()` is shared by BM25 and SQLite FTS flows so lexical matching stays aligned across search channels.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:722:Watcher- and ingest-triggered reindex paths now use the normal synchronous embedding cache-miss flow. Deferred embeddings remain opt-in via `asyncEmbedding` or failure fallback.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:725:`memory_index_scan` now runs the broader post-mutation invalidation hook behavior whenever a scan indexes, updates, or stale-deletes rows.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:737:Typed-degree ranking is cached per database connection and invalidated through `clearDegreeCache()` / `clearDegreeCacheForDb()` after causal-edge and mutation flows, so the degree channel stays in sync with recent graph writes.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:757:Selection tracking writes to a separate `learned_triggers` column (NOT FTS5 index). 10 safeguards: separate column, 30-day TTL, 100+ stop words denylist, rate cap (3 terms/selection, 8 terms/memory), top-3 exclusion, 1-week shadow period, <72h memory exclusion, sprint gate review, rollback mechanism, provenance audit log. Query weight: **0.7x** of organic triggers. Gated via `SPECKIT_LEARN_FROM_SELECTION` (default ON; set to `false` to disable).\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:772:import Database from 'better-sqlite3';\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:914:| `better-sqlite3` | SQLite database driver  |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:915:| `sqlite-vec`     | Vector search extension |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:921:| Provides BFS connected-component labelling, single-level Louvain modularity and momentum/depth scoring.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:33:The graph module operates on the `causal_edges` and `memory_index` tables to detect communities, compute scoring signals, manage temporal edge validity, detect contradictions, track usage, and generate community summaries. All features are independently gated behind `SPECKIT_*` feature flags.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:38:- **Community summaries** generate readable text summaries per community by aggregating member titles and extracting top topics. Stored in `community_summaries` table.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:40:- **Graph signals** compute two additive score bonuses for search results: momentum (degree change over 7 days) and causal depth (normalized longest-path depth on the SCC-condensed causal DAG).\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:41:- **Temporal edges** add `valid_at`/`invalid_at` columns to `causal_edges` for bi-temporal validity management.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:52:| Session-scoped caching | Momentum and depth caches prevent redundant DB queries within a single session. Caches clear at 10,000 entries. |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:69:  graph-signals.ts            # Momentum and causal depth scoring, degree (sqlite-vec)       -> Semantic matches\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:93:| Vector | `vector-index.ts` | Semantic similarity via sqlite-vec through the split Graph signals (N2a + N2b) — momentum + causal depth\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:111:8. Feedback signals — learned trigger boosts (0.7x weight) + negative demotions\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:143:- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:177:- `lexicalPath`: the lane that actually ran for lexical retrieval. Current packet-owned values are `fts5` and `unavailable`. The wider response schema also allows `like`, but packet `010` does not claim that lane for this runtime seam.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:184:| `ok` | FTS5 compile probe passed, `memory_fts` exists, and BM25 ranking executed normally | `fts5` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:187:| `no_such_module_fts5` | The SQLite engine rejects FTS5 usage with `no such module: fts5` | `unavailable` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:297:| `causal-boost.ts`          | -      | TypeScript | Causal-neighbor score boosting for graph traversal  |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:298:| `session-boost.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Session-attention score boosting                    |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:311:| `sqlite-fts.ts`            | -      | TypeScript | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring, extracted from hybrid-search for independent use |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:319:| `learned-feedback.ts`      | -      | TypeScript | Selection-based relevance feedback with learned triggers (R11) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:347:| `session-state.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Tracks per-session search state and retrieval context |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:348:| `session-transition.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Handles session-state transition rules during retrieval flows |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:383:   MMR diversity reranking -> Reduce near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:427:| `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS`   | `true`  | Enable N2a momentum + N2b causal depth scoring |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:451:| v3      | Add `related_memories` column to `memory_index`                              |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:456:| v8      | `causal_edges` table with 6 relationship types (REQ-012, T043-T047)          |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:475:// Float32Array -> Buffer conversion for sqlite-vec\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:612:**Purpose**: Create causal edges between memories that share extracted entities across spec folders. Gated via `SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING`. Requires R10 (`SPECKIT_AUTO_ENTITIES`) to also be enabled.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:620:| **Edge Creation** | Creates `supports` causal edges between memories sharing entities |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:631:| `createEntityLinks` | `(db, matches) => EntityLinkResult` | Create causal edges for cross-doc matches |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:646:| `caused` | 1.0 | Strongest causal signal |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:650:| `supersedes` | 0.6 | Version supersession |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:656:Top-ranked results seed a 2-hop BFS traversal over causal edges. Discovered neighbors receive a score boost with `SEED_FRACTION=0.25` strength. A `1/sqrt(neighbor_count)` fan-effect divisor (R17) prevents hub nodes from appearing in every co-activation result.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:662:The causal graph is condensed into strongly connected components, then longest-path depth is computed across the resulting DAG. Shortcut edges do not collapse deeper chains; cycle members share one bounded depth layer, and the final depth is normalized by the deepest reachable component chain (`maxDepth`). Score bonus: `normalizedDepth * 0.05` — rewards structurally deep nodes in the causal chain.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:673:Processing steps applied during `memory_save` before a memory is persisted.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:687:| 2. Content Quality | 5-dimension weighted signal density | Dimensions: title (0.25), triggers (0.20), length (0.20), anchors (0.15), metadata (0.20) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:688:| 3. Semantic Dedup | Cosine similarity against existing memories | Threshold: 0.92 rejects near-duplicates |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:698:| 0.75 - 0.88 | **Conflict** | Supersede: deprecate existing, create `supersedes` causal edge |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:719:`buildBm25DocumentText()` builds the canonical lexical document from title, content, trigger phrases, and folder metadata. `normalizeLexicalQueryTokens()` is shared by BM25 and SQLite FTS flows so lexical matching stays aligned across search channels.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:722:Watcher- and ingest-triggered reindex paths now use the normal synchronous embedding cache-miss flow. Deferred embeddings remain opt-in via `asyncEmbedding` or failure fallback.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:725:`memory_index_scan` now runs the broader post-mutation invalidation hook behavior whenever a scan indexes, updates, or stale-deletes rows.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:737:Typed-degree ranking is cached per database connection and invalidated through `clearDegreeCache()` / `clearDegreeCacheForDb()` after causal-edge and mutation flows, so the degree channel stays in sync with recent graph writes.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:757:Selection tracking writes to a separate `learned_triggers` column (NOT FTS5 index). 10 safeguards: separate column, 30-day TTL, 100+ stop words denylist, rate cap (3 terms/selection, 8 terms/memory), top-3 exclusion, 1-week shadow period, <72h memory exclusion, sprint gate review, rollback mechanism, provenance audit log. Query weight: **0.7x** of organic triggers. Gated via `SPECKIT_LEARN_FROM_SELECTION` (default ON; set to `false` to disable).\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:772:import Database from 'better-sqlite3';\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:914:| `better-sqlite3` | SQLite database driver The second repair extracted a shared truncation helper so long session summaries stop at a clean word boundary and carry the canonical Unicode ellipsis instead of drifting between punctuation styles. The fixture describes a realistic verification pass where the helper was reused by the observation summary path, the OVERVIEW owner adopted the same contract, the compiled CLI replay was exercised /bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/changelog/13--sk-deep-review/v1.3.0.0.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	## [**1.3.0.0**] - 2026-04-11
     2	
     3	This release lands Phase 008 of the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle. `sk-deep-review` now actively utilizes the coverage graph on the live review loop, persists blocked-stop bundles through reducer-owned surfaces, fails loud (instead of silent) on malformed JSONL and missing machine-owned anchors, and splits `repeatedFindings` into semantically distinct `persistentSameSeverity` + `severityChanged` buckets for precise triage.
     4	
     5	---
     6	
     7	### Contract Truth (Part A)
     8	
     9	- `step_emit_blocked_stop` wires a first-class `blocked_stop` JSONL event into both review workflows with review-specific gate names: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`. Each event carries the full `blockedBy` / `gateResults` / `recoveryStrategy` payload.
    10	- `step_normalize_pause_events` rewrites raw `paused` → `userPaused` and `stuck_recovery` → `stuckRecovery` at emission time using the frozen `STOP_REASONS` enum.
    11	- The reducer no longer drops non-iteration events. `buildBlockedStopHistory()` scans `blocked_stop` rows (with backward-compat for the legacy nested `legalStop` payload shape) and exposes `blockedStopHistory` as a registry field.
    12	
    13	---
    14	
    15	### Graph Wiring (Part B)
    16	
    17	- `spec_kit_deep-review_{auto,confirm}.yaml` now call `deep_loop_graph_upsert` after `step_reduce_review_state` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` before the inline review convergence vote in `step_check_convergence`. Combined stop rule: STOP requires BOTH inline vote and graph decision to agree. `STOP_BLOCKED` routes to the blocked-stop emission path.
    18	- `buildGraphConvergenceRollup(records)` scans `graph_convergence` JSONL events and exposes `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, `graphBlockers` as new registry fields alongside `convergenceScore`.
    19	- Shared coverage-graph reads are now session-scoped: `coverage-graph-query.ts`, `convergence.ts`, `query.ts`, `status.ts` all accept and propagate `sessionId`. Review packets with multiple concurrent sessions no longer bleed across each other's graph state.
    20	
    21	---
    22	
    23	### Reducer Fail-Closed Hardening (Part C)
    24	
    25	- `parseJsonl()` is now backed by `parseJsonlDetailed()` which collects malformed lines into a `corruptionWarnings` array instead of silently dropping them. `parseJsonl` still returns the plain records array for backward compatibility.
    26	- `reduceReviewState()` now emits a warning to stderr when `corruptionWarnings.length > 0` and exits with code `2`. Pass `--lenient` (CLI) or `lenient:true` (function option) to preserve the v1.2.0.0 fail-open behavior for legacy packets.
    27	- `replaceAnchorSection()` now throws `Error('Missing machine-owned anchor "<id>" in deep-review strategy file...')` when a required anchor is absent. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or `createMissingAnchors:true`) to bootstrap missing anchor blocks from scratch.
    28	- `review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` validates all three paths: corruption detection, missing anchor throw, and `--lenient` escape hatch (3/3 passing).
    29	
    30	---
    31	
    32	### repeatedFindings Split (REQ-018)
    33	
    34	- The `repeatedFindings` registry field is now **deprecated**. It's retained as a backward-compat union of the two new arrays:
    35	  - `persistentSameSeverity` — findings observed in ≥2 iterations with no severity transitions (only the initial discovery entry).
    36	  - `severityChanged` — findings that went through ≥1 severity transition (P0↔P1↔P2) in their `transitions` history.
    37	- Dashboard renders both new counters in the TREND section, making triage at scale (50+ findings) possible without conflating "same finding reappeared" with "finding's severity was upgraded".
    38	
    39	---
    40	
    41	### Reducer Surfacing (Part C)
    42	
    43	- Dashboard gained new sections: `BLOCKED STOPS`, `GRAPH CONVERGENCE`, `CORRUPTION WARNINGS`. Each entry is rendered with timestamps, gate result summaries, and recovery strategy hints.
    44	- Strategy `next-focus` anchor prefers the latest blocked-stop `recoveryStrategy` when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event — operators see the blocker before choosing next-iteration direction.
    45	
    46	---
    47	
    48	### Fixtures and Regression Coverage
    49	
    50	- New fixture: `scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/` — a 3-iteration review packet with a full legal-stop blocked bundle, including one severity upgrade (F002: P2 → P1) and two persistent same-severity findings (F001 active P0, F003 active P1). The pre-generated dashboard shows the BLOCKED STOPS section rendering iteration 3 with full gate summary.
    51	- `session-isolation.vitest.ts` validates that two concurrent review sessions in the same packet cannot see each other's graph nodes (3/3 passing).
    52	- `review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` validates corruption detection + missing anchor throw + `--lenient` escape hatch (3/3 passing).
    53	- New playbook scenarios under `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/`: `022-blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` and `023-fail-closed-reducer.md`.
    54	
    55	---
    56	
    57	### Verification
    58	
    59	- Phase 008 vitest run (4 suites, 12 tests): **all passing**. `graph-convergence-parity` (3/3), `session-isolation` (3/3), `review-reducer-fail-closed` (3/3), `graph-aware-stop` (3/3).
    60	- Reducer smoke test on blocked-stop-session fixture: `blockedStopHistory` populated with 1 entry, `severityChanged` with 1 (F002), `persistentSameSeverity` with 2 (F001 + F003), dashboard BLOCKED STOPS section renders iteration 3 with full gate summary.
    61	- Malformed JSONL smoke test: without `--lenient` exits 2 with corruption preview on stderr; with `--lenient` exits 0 and still reports corruption count.
    62	- Idempotency preserved.
    63	
    64	---
    65	
    66	## Files Changed
    67	
    68	| File | Action | Description |
    69	| ---- | ------ | ----------- |
    70	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md` | Modified | Bumped version to 1.3.0.0 |

    72	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` | Modified | Legal-stop → blocked_stop emission contract |
    73	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Modified | parseJsonlDetailed + fail-closed JSONL + fail-closed anchors + blockedStopHistory + graphConvergenceScore + persistentSameSeverity + severityChanged + dashboard sections |
    74	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/*` | Created | Durable fixture with full blocked_stop bundle, severity upgrade, persistent findings |
    75	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/022-blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Created | Manual test for blocked-stop surfacing |
    76	| `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/023-fail-closed-reducer.md` | Created | Manual test for fail-closed behavior |
    77	| `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml` | Modified | Wired step_emit_blocked_stop + step_normalize_pause_events + step_graph_upsert + step_graph_convergence |
    78	| `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml` | Modified | Mirrored the same contract in confirm mode |
    79	| `.opencode/command/spec_kit/deep-review.md` | Modified | Provisioned `code_graph_query` + `code_graph_context` in LEAF-tool budget |
    80	| `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md` | Modified | Added explicit graph tool permissions |
    81	| `.claude/agents/deep-review.md` | Modified | Synced graph tool permissions |
    82	| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` | Created | Validates corruption detection + missing anchor throw + --lenient escape hatch |
    83	
    84	---
    85	
    86	## Upgrade
    87	
    88	**Backward-compatible for existing packets** as long as JSONL files are clean and strategy files have all machine-owned anchors. If a packet was authored under v1.2.0.0 and contains:
    89	- Malformed JSONL lines → the v1.3.0.0 reducer will exit non-zero. Pass `--lenient` to preserve the prior fail-open behavior while still reporting corruption in the registry.
    90	- Missing machine-owned strategy anchors → the v1.3.0.0 reducer will throw. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or create the missing anchor blocks manually) to bootstrap.
    91	- `repeatedFindings` consumers → the field is retained as a deprecated union of `persistentSameSeverity` + `severityChanged`. New code should read the split arrays directly.
    92	
    93	No migration is required for packets created under v1.3.0.0 or later.

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:249:#### findingStability (0.0-1.0)
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:273:- `findingStability >= 0.85` supports STOP: the finding set has stabilized and further iterations are unlikely to materially change the review outcome.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:274:- `findingStability < 0.50` prevents STOP: the finding set is still in flux and the review has not reached a stable assessment.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:280:1. **findingStability gate** (existing): The existing `findingStability` gate already evaluates rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio. The new `semanticNovelty` signal adds a sub-check:
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:284:2. **findingStability signal** (new): The `findingStability` metric (0.0-1.0) is surfaced alongside the existing convergence signals. It supports the `findingStability` gate evaluation by providing a registry-level stability measure that complements the ratio-based churn signals.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:286:The gate passes only when both the existing churn-based checks AND the semantic stability checks agree. When a semantic check fails, the `legalStop.gateResults.findingStability` detail string includes the semantic signal values.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:298:    "findingStability": { "value": 0.92, "supportsStop": true }
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:320:Semantic convergence signals (`semanticNovelty`, `findingStability`) require at least 2 evidence iterations to produce meaningful values. They are omitted from legal-stop evaluation when insufficient data exists.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:377:| **findingStability** | Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio must all indicate low-yield review churn | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:379:| **p0Resolution** | No unresolved P0 findings may remain active at stop time | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:380:| **evidenceDensity** | Evidence density across active findings must meet the configured threshold | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:381:| **hotspotSaturation** | Review hotspots must be revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |

If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class blocked-stop decision with `stopReason=blockedStop`, populate `legalStop.blockedBy` from the failing gates, copy the full `gateResults`, snapshot the replay inputs, and attach a `recoveryStrategy` describing the next review action before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:426:| `findingStability` | Revisit the noisiest recent dimension and reduce novelty by closing obvious follow-up loops before re-checking STOP. |

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:430:| `hotspotSaturation` | Revisit undersampled hotspots or adjacent call sites until the saturation heuristic passes. |

     1	---
     2	name: deep-research
     3	description: "Autonomous deep research agent executing single iteration cycles with externalized state"
     4	mode: subagent
     5	temperature: 0.1
     6	permission:
     7	  read: allow
     8	  write: allow
     9	  edit: allow
    10	  bash: allow
    11	  grep: allow
    12	  glob: allow
    13	  webfetch: allow
    14	  memory: allow
    15	  code_graph_query: allow
    16	  code_graph_context: allow
    17	  chrome_devtools: deny
    18	  task: deny
    19	  list: allow
    20	  patch: deny
    21	  external_directory: allow
    22	---
    23	
    24	# The Deep Researcher: Autonomous Iteration Agent
    25	
    26	Executes ONE research iteration within an autonomous loop. Reads externalized state, performs focused research, writes findings to files, and updates state for the next iteration.
    27	
    28	**Path Convention**: Use only `.opencode/agent/*.md` as the canonical runtime path reference.
    29	
    30	**CRITICAL**: This agent executes a SINGLE iteration, not the full loop. The loop is managed by the `/spec_kit:deep-research` command's YAML workflow. This agent is dispatched once per iteration with explicit context about what to investigate.
    31	
    32	**IMPORTANT**: This agent is research-focused and codebase-agnostic. Adapts investigation approach based on the topic and available tools.
    33	
    34	> **SPEC FOLDER PERMISSION:** @deep-research has explicit permission to write the `research/` packet, including `research/research.md`, inside spec folders. This is an exception to the @speckit exclusivity rule because deep-research produces iteration artifacts and progressive research synthesis.
    35	
    36	---
    37	
    38	## 0. ILLEGAL NESTING (HARD BLOCK)
    39	
    40	This agent is LEAF-only. Nested sub-agent dispatch is illegal.
    41	- NEVER create sub-tasks or dispatch sub-agents.
    42	- NEVER use the Task tool.
    43	- If a research question requires delegation, document it in findings and recommend it for a future iteration.
    44	- All research actions must be self-contained within this single execution.
    45	
    46	---
    47	
    48	## 1. CORE WORKFLOW
    49	
    50	### Single Iteration Protocol
    51	
    52	Every iteration follows this exact sequence:
    53	
    54	```
    55	1. READ STATE ──────> Read JSONL + strategy.md
    56	2. DETERMINE FOCUS ─> Pick focus from strategy "Next Focus"
    57	3. EXECUTE RESEARCH ─> 3-5 research actions (WebFetch, Grep, Read, memory_search)
    58	4. WRITE FINDINGS ──> Create research/iterations/iteration-NNN.md
    59	5. APPEND STATE ────> Add iteration record to JSONL
    60	6. REDUCER SYNC ────> Workflow refreshes strategy, registry, dashboard
    61	7. UPDATE RESEARCH ─> Progressively update research/research.md (if exists)
    62	```
    63	
    64	### Step-by-Step Detail
    65	
    66	#### Step 1: Read State
    67	Read these files (paths provided in dispatch context):
    68	- `research/deep-research-state.jsonl` -- Understand iteration history
    69	- `research/deep-research-strategy.md` -- Understand what to investigate
    70	- `research/findings-registry.json` (if exists) -- Understand open/resolved questions and key findings
    71	- `research/research-ideas.md` (if exists) -- Deferred ideas and promising tangents
    72	
    73	Extract from state:
    74	- Current iteration number (count JSONL iteration records + 1)
    75	- Remaining key questions
    76	- Exhausted approaches (DO NOT retry these)
    77	- Recommended next focus
    78	- Lifecycle branch from `config.lineage.lineageMode` (`new`, `resume`, or `restart`). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred — see `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.
    79	
    80	#### Step 2: Determine Focus

    22	
    23	# The Deep Reviewer: Iterative Code Quality Agent
    24	
    25	Executes ONE review iteration within an autonomous review loop. Reads externalized state, reviews code quality across one dimension, produces P0/P1/P2 findings with file:line evidence, and updates state for the next iteration.
    26	
    27	**Path Convention**: Use only `.opencode/agent/*.md` as the canonical runtime path reference.
    28	
    29	**CRITICAL**: This agent executes a SINGLE review iteration, not the full loop. The loop is managed by the `/spec_kit:deep-review` command's YAML workflow. This agent is dispatched once per iteration with explicit context about what dimension to review.
    30	
    31	**IMPORTANT**: This agent is a hybrid of @review (quality rubric, severity classification, adversarial self-check) and the deep-review loop contract (state protocol, JSONL, lifecycle continuity). It reviews code but does NOT modify it.
    32	
    33	> **SPEC FOLDER PERMISSION:** @deep-review may write only `review/` artifacts inside the active spec folder (iteration artifacts, strategy, JSONL, dashboard, report). Review target files are strictly READ-ONLY, and writes outside `review/` are not part of this agent contract.
    34	
    35	---
    36	
    37	## 0. ILLEGAL NESTING (HARD BLOCK)
    38	
    39	This agent is LEAF-only. Nested sub-agent dispatch is illegal.
    40	- NEVER create sub-tasks or dispatch sub-agents.
    41	- NEVER use the Task tool.
    42	- If a review question requires delegation, document it in findings and recommend it for a future iteration.
    43	- All review actions must be self-contained within this single execution.
    44	
    45	---
    46	
    47	## 1. CORE WORKFLOW -- Single Review Iteration
    48	
    49	Every iteration follows this exact sequence:
    50	
    51	```
    52	1. READ STATE ──────> Read JSONL + strategy + config
    53	2. DETERMINE FOCUS ─> Select dimension from strategy "Next Focus"
    54	3. EXECUTE REVIEW ──> 3-5 analysis actions (Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)
    55	4. CLASSIFY FINDINGS > Assign P0/P1/P2 with file:line evidence
    56	5. WRITE FINDINGS ──> Create review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md
    57	6. UPDATE STRATEGY ─> Edit review/deep-review-strategy.md sections
    58	7. APPEND JSONL ────> Add ONE iteration record
    59	```
    60	
    61	### Step-by-Step Detail
    62	
    63	#### Step 1: Read State
    64	Read these files (paths provided in dispatch context):
    65	- `review/deep-review-state.jsonl` -- Understand iteration history
    66	- `review/deep-review-findings-registry.json` -- Read reducer-owned active finding state (read-only for this agent)
    67	- `review/deep-review-strategy.md` -- Understand what dimensions to review
    68	- `review/deep-review-config.json` -- Read review configuration, lineage metadata, and release readiness state (read-only)
    69	
    70	Extract from state:
    71	- Current iteration number (count JSONL iteration records + 1)
    72	- Dimensions completed vs remaining
    73	- Prior findings (P0/P1/P2 counts)
    74	- Exhausted approaches (DO NOT retry these)
    75	- Recommended next focus
    76	- Stuck count
    77	
    78	#### Step 2: Determine Focus
    79	
    80	**MANDATORY PRE-CHECK**: Before choosing a focus, read strategy.md "Exhausted Approaches" section:

 succeeded in 0ms:
    80	  },
    81	  "recoveryStrategy": "Dispatch the next iteration at the maintainability dimension and re-check after resolving the remaining P0.",
    82	  "timestamp": "2026-03-24T15:02:00Z",
    83	  "sessionId": "rvw-2026-03-24T10-00-00Z",
    84	  "generation": 1
    85	}
    86	```
    87	
    88	- `blockedBy`: array of gate names that failed (string[] — never structured objects). Empty when STOP is legal, in which case no `blocked_stop` event is emitted.
    89	- `gateResults`: named sub-records keyed by `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, and `claimAdjudicationGate`. Each sub-record has a `pass` boolean plus gate-specific fields (score, covered/missing, activeP0, avgEvidencePerFinding, activeP0P1). The reducer reads these verbatim and does not coerce shapes.
    90	- `recoveryStrategy`: human-readable one-liner describing what the next iteration should do before another stop attempt.
    91	- When the graph convergence verdict is `STOP_BLOCKED`, the same event is emitted with `blocked_gate == "graph_blockers"` folded into `blockedBy` and the structured blocker objects preserved under `graph_blocker_detail_json` (see phase 008 P1-02 closure in the auto YAML).
    92	
    93	### Decision Priority
    94	
    95	Checks are evaluated in this order (first match wins):
    96	
    97	1. **Max iterations** -- hard cap, always checked first.
    98	2. **All dimensions covered + clean** -- all 4 dimensions covered, no active P0/P1, stabilization passed, gates passed.
    99	3. **Stuck detection** -- 2+ consecutive no-progress iterations.
   100	4. **Composite convergence** -- 3-signal weighted vote with threshold 0.60.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   371	## 6. LEGAL-STOP GATE BUNDLE
   372	
   373	Deep review treats STOP as legal only when the full review-specific gate bundle passes together. Convergence math may request STOP, but the workflow must still evaluate these 5 gates and persist a blocked-stop event when any gate fails.
   374	
   375	| Gate | Rule | Fail Action |
   376	|------|------|-------------|
   377	| **findingStability** | Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio must all indicate low-yield review churn | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   378	| **dimensionCoverage** | Every configured review dimension must have been examined at least once, with required traceability coverage stabilized | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   379	| **p0Resolution** | No unresolved P0 findings may remain active at stop time | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   380	| **evidenceDensity** | Evidence density across active findings must meet the configured threshold | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   381	| **hotspotSaturation** | Review hotspots must be revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
   382	
   383	### Gate Evaluation
   384	
   385	```
   386	function buildReviewLegalStop(state, config, coverage):
   387	  gateResults = {
   388	    findingStability: {
   389	      pass: rollingStop and madStop and state.latestNoveltyRatio <= config.convergenceThreshold,
   390	      detail: "Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio are all below stop thresholds."
   391	    },
   392	    dimensionCoverage: {
   393	      pass: everyConfiguredDimensionExaminedAtLeastOnce(coverage, config.reviewDimensions) and
   394	            coverage.requiredProtocolsCovered and
   395	            coverage.stabilizationPasses >= 1,
   396	      detail: "All configured review dimensions have been examined, required traceability protocols are covered, and stabilization has aged enough to stop."
   397	    },
   398	    p0Resolution: {
   399	      pass: countActiveFindings(state, ["P0"]) == 0 and state.claimAdjudicationPassed != false,
   400	      detail: "No unresolved P0 findings remain and blocker adjudication is complete."
   401	    },
   402	    evidenceDensity: {
   403	      pass: computeEvidenceDensity(state.activeFindings) >= config.evidenceDensityThreshold,
   404	      detail: "Evidence density meets the configured threshold for active findings."
   405	    },
   406	    hotspotSaturation: {
   407	      pass: computeHotspotSaturation(state.hotspots) >= config.hotspotSaturationThreshold,
   408	      detail: "Priority hotspots received enough revisits to satisfy saturation."
   409	    }
   410	  }
   411	
   412	  blockedBy = [name for name, result in gateResults.items() if not result.pass]
   413	  return {
   414	    pass: len(blockedBy) == 0,
   415	    blockedBy,
   416	    gateResults
   417	  }
   418	```
   419	
   420	When convergence math returns STOP, invoke `buildReviewLegalStop()`. If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class blocked-stop decision with `stopReason=blockedStop`, populate `legalStop.blockedBy` from the failing gates, copy the full `gateResults`, snapshot the replay inputs, and attach a `recoveryStrategy` describing the next review action before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
   421

   464	function buildBlockedStopHistory(records) {
   465	  return records
   466	    .filter((record) => record?.type === 'event' && record?.event === 'blocked_stop')
   467	    .map((record) => {
   468	      const legacyLegalStop = record.legalStop && typeof record.legalStop === 'object'
