codex
**Findings**
1. `F-016-001` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability` | File: `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md` | Line: `148-149`, `188-213`
Title: Parser-sensitive iteration skeleton is internally contradictory

Description: The canonical deep-review runtime mirror says the iteration markdown shape is parser-sensitive and deviations can silently drop findings, but the example immediately diverges into two competing templates. The fenced example closes at line 201, then a second `## Assessment` block plus `## Reflection` appears outside the example with an extra trailing fence. The reducer only extracts one `Assessment` section and a fixed set of headings, so this prompt now gives future maintainers and the model two incompatible shapes for the same reducer-owned artifact.

Evidence: `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md:148-149` warns that the reducer at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:186` depends on exact headings; `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md:188-213` then duplicates `## Assessment` and leaves a stray fence. The reducer only reads `Focus`, `Findings`, `Ruled Out`, `Dead Ends`, `Recommended Next Focus`, and `Assessment` via `extractSection()` / `parseIterationFile()` in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:109-119` and `186-220`.

Recommendation: Collapse this to one canonical fenced template, keep only the sections the reducer actually consumes, and add a parity test that asserts the runtime mirror’s section order and headings.

2. `F-016-002` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability` | File: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml`, `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml` | Line: `446-453`, `486`; `479-486`, `519`
Title: `STOP_BLOCKED` review events drop the structured graph-blocker payload they claim to preserve

Description: Both review workflows document that graph blockers should be normalized to `blockedBy: string[]` while preserving the full blocker objects for operator inspection. That preservation never happens in the emitted `blocked_stop` event. As a result, once the workflow hits the `STOP_BLOCKED` branch, only the blocker names survive; the richer graph blocker metadata is lost before the reducer can surface it. That weakens the phase-008 “graph-aware blocked stop” contract and makes future diagnostics harder.

Evidence: The auto workflow explicitly says “preserve the full objects in `graph_blocker_detail_json`” at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:446-453`, but `step_emit_blocked_stop` at line `486` does not serialize any `graphBlockerDetail` field. The confirm workflow has the same promise at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:479-486` and the same omission at line `519`. The reducer is already prepared to read `record.graphBlockerDetail` in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:472-476`, and the reference contract promises those objects are preserved in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:88-91`.

Recommendation: Add `graphBlockerDetail` to the emitted `blocked_stop` payload in both workflows, thread it through the step outputs explicitly, and add a review-side fixture/test that asserts structured blocker detail survives a `STOP_BLOCKED` run.

3. `F-016-003` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `maintainability` | File: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml`, `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml` | Line: `247-248`, `268-274`; `255-256`, `276-282`
Title: `reviewDimensions` normalization still has two separate workflow sources of truth

Description: The Phase 008 pre-serialization fix correctly normalizes `review_dimensions_json` before writing the JSONL config row, but the canonical `deep-review-config.json` path still populates `reviewDimensions` from raw `{review_dimensions}`. That means the workflow now maintains two independent representations of the same field, while the documented schema says the field is always `string[]`. Even if current packet generation happens to produce an array, future input-shape changes now have to be kept in sync manually across both write paths.

Evidence: In auto mode, `step_create_config` still writes `reviewDimensions: "{review_dimensions}"` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:247-248`, while `step_create_state_log` separately normalizes `review_dimensions_json` at lines `268-274`. Confirm mode mirrors the same split at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:255-256` and `276-282`. The contract docs still define `reviewDimensions` as `string[]` in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:52-58` and `106-112`.

Recommendation: Normalize once during initialization and reuse the same parsed array for both `deep-review-config.json` and the JSONL config row.

4. `F-016-004` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `maintainability` | File: `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md` | Line: `55-61`, `67-78`, `205-212`
Title: Deep-research runtime mirror still treats config as a late read even though early behavior depends on it

Description: The deep-research runtime mirror still presents Step 1 as “read JSONL + strategy.md,” and its mandatory file list omits `research/deep-research-config.json`. But the same section tells the agent to reason about `config.lineage.lineageMode`, and later Step 7 says the agent must read config before deciding whether progressive synthesis may touch `research/research.md`. That split is no longer a faithful runtime contract and makes the leaf instructions harder to maintain in sync with the workflow.

Evidence: The sequence at `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:55-61` says Step 1 reads only JSONL + strategy. The explicit Step 1 file list at lines `67-71` omits config, yet line `78` asks the agent to derive lifecycle state from `config.lineage.lineageMode`. Later, Step 7 requires reading config before touching `research/research.md` at `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:205-212`.

Recommendation: Make `research/deep-research-config.json` an explicit Step 1 input and move `lineageMode` / `progressiveSynthesis` extraction into the initial state-read contract.

**Iteration Summary**
Total findings: `4`
Breakdown: `0 P0`, `2 P1`, `2 P2`

I did not find a direct arithmetic defect in the inline 3-signal threshold math during this pass. The maintainability problems were in the contracts around that logic: the parser-facing iteration scaffold, the blocked-stop evidence retention path, and the surrounding runtime/documentation alignment.

**Coverage Assessment**
Reviewed about `90%` of the requested focus area: `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md`, `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs`, both deep-review workflow YAMLs, and the supporting convergence/state-format/changelog surfaces tied to Phase 007-008. I did not do a full mirror sweep of `.claude/`, `.gemini/`, and `.codex` deep-review/deep-research variants in this iteration.

**Confidence**
`high`

**Next Priorities**
1. Mirror-parity sweep for `.claude/agents/deep-review.md`, `.gemini/agents/deep-review.md`, and `.codex/agents/deep-review.toml` to confirm the same parser/template and gate-contract drift does not persist there.
2. Add or inspect executable coverage for the review-side `step_check_convergence` decision tree, especially `STOP_BLOCKED` plus `claimAdjudicationGate`, because current tests are much stronger on reducer/schema parity than on the live review workflow branch behavior.
3. Check whether the same config-read drift exists in non-OpenCode deep-research runtime mirrors and command docs, since `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md` still understates config’s role in the iteration contract.

Evidence: In auto mode, `step_create_config` still writes `reviewDimensions: "{review_dimensions}"` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:247-248`, while `step_create_state_log` separately normalizes `review_dimensions_json` at lines `268-274`. The contract docs still define `reviewDimensions` as `string[]` in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:52-58` and `106-112`.

Recommendation: Normalize once during initialization and reuse the same parsed array for both `deep-review-config.json` and the JSONL config row.

4. `F-016-004` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `maintainability` | File: `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md` | Line: `55-61`, `67-78`, `205-212`
Title: Deep-research runtime mirror still treats config as a late read even though early behavior depends on it

Description: The deep-research runtime mirror still presents Step 1 as “read JSONL + strategy.md,” and its mandatory file list omits `research/deep-research-config.json`. But the same section tells the agent to reason about `config.lineage.lineageMode`, and later Step 7 says the agent must read config before deciding whether progressive synthesis may touch `research/research.md`. That split is no longer a faithful runtime contract and makes the leaf instructions harder to maintain in sync with the workflow.

Evidence: The sequence at `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:55-61` says Step 1 reads only JSONL + strategy. The explicit Step 1 file list at lines `67-71` omits config, yet line `78` asks the agent to derive lifecycle state from `config.lineage.lineageMode`. Later, Step 7 requires reading config before touching `research/research.md` at `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:205-212`.

Recommendation: Make `research/deep-research-config.json` an explicit Step 1 input and move `lineageMode` / `progressiveSynthesis` extraction into the initial state-read contract.

**Iteration Summary**
Total findings: `4`
Breakdown: `0 P0`, `2 P1`, `2 P2`

I did not find a direct arithmetic defect in the inline 3-signal threshold math during this pass. The maintainability problems were in the contracts around that logic: the parser-facing iteration scaffold, the blocked-stop evidence retention path, and the surrounding runtime/documentation alignment.

**Coverage Assessment**
Reviewed about `90%` of the requested focus area: `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md`, `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs`, both deep-review workflow YAMLs, and the supporting convergence/state-format/changelog surfaces tied to Phase 007-008. I did not do a full mirror sweep of `.claude/`, `.gemini/`, and `.codex` deep-review/deep-research variants in this iteration.

**Confidence**
`high`

**Next Priorities**
1. Mirror-parity sweep for `.claude/agents/deep-review.md`, `.gemini/agents/deep-review.md`, and `.codex/agents/deep-review.toml` to confirm the same parser/template and gate-contract drift does not persist there.
2. Add or inspect executable coverage for the review-side `step_check_convergence` decision tree, especially `STOP_BLOCKED` plus `claimAdjudicationGate`, because current tests are much stronger on reducer/schema parity than on the live review workflow branch behavior.
3. Check whether the same config-read drift exists in non-OpenCode deep-research runtime mirrors and command docs, since `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md` still understates config’s role in the iteration contract.
